milestone_run.input_mode: raw bitstream bytes with FPGA-side downsampling

actual_observed.measurement_scope: CoyoteOverlay.predict_raw(...) wraps only coyote_lib.predict(model) with time.time_ns(); it excludes raw file loading, CPU Keras inference, raw input packing/copy into Coyote buffers, flush/get output copies, validation CSV writing, and FPGA programming.
actual_observed.note: The copied validation log lost the leading digits of batch 3 latency and only preserved '.593us'; 64618.593us is the value recorded from the run/console and is consistent with the reported 247.6 samples/s throughput for batch size 16.

top_model_wrapper.reason_latency_is_variable: The raw-input downsampler consumes a runtime raw_len from the first AXI beat, so Vitis HLS cannot report one fixed top-level latency for model_wrapper.

data_to_axi_stream.note: The output adapter is included in top-level resources but not broken out in this copied report's summary table.

raw_bitstream_downsample_to_input_stream.reason_latency_is_variable: The large-file path loops over ceil(raw_len / 64) AXI beats at runtime.

timing_post_route.status: All user specified timing constraints are met.

instruction_count_after_patch.interpretation: The raw downsampler adapter itself is small after synthesis: 320 HW-transform instructions. The remaining design size is dominated by the unchanged zero_in hls4ml CNN.
